D3 Chemistry L4 Analyze Reference

Kubelka-Munk Coefficients K and S

two spectral coefficients describe a pigment film: K(λ) absorption per unit thickness, S(λ) scattering per unit thickness; reflectance follows R∞ = 1 + K/S − √((K/S)(K/S+2))

The Kubelka-Munk model treats a thick paint film as two diffuse fluxes (down and up). Each pigment contributes K(λ) absorbance per unit and S(λ) scattering per unit. At infinite thickness the reflectance is R∞ = 1 + K/S − √((K/S)(K/S+2)). Pure white pigments have low K and high S; pure black pigments have high K and low S; the ratio K/S is what colour-matching software actually fits.

Pick a pigment from the dropdown; the K(λ) and S(λ) curves plot across 400-700 nm and the R∞(λ) reflectance follows the K-M equation. The strip swatch is the integrated colour you'd see.
